parts.description: An optional arrow element pointing toward the active trigger.
An optional backdrop rendered behind the popup.

The content shown when its item is open. Used as a structural directive; its template is rendered
into the shared {@link RdxNavigationMenuViewport}.

```html
<ng-container *rdxNavigationMenuContent>…</ng-container>
```

inputs.name: forceMount
inputs.description: Keep the content mounted in the viewport even when its item is closed.

inputs.name: rdxNavigationMenuContent
inputs.description: Required by the structural directive syntax; the value is unused.

parts.description:
A visual indicator (e.g. a caret) rendered inside a trigger. Exposes the open state so the icon
can rotate when its item opens.
A single navigation menu item. Holds a trigger + content pair, or a standalone link.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: A unique value that identifies the item. Falls back to a stable generated id.

parts.description:
A navigation link. Can close the menu on selection when `closeOnClick` is enabled.

Used both as a top-level navigation item and inside content. Top-level links join the list's
composite collection, matching Base UI's CompositeItem-backed NavigationMenu.Link.

inputs.name: active
inputs.description: Whether the link represents the current page.

inputs.name: closeOnClick
inputs.description: Whether selecting the link should close the menu.

outputs.name: onSelect
outputs.description: Emits when the link is selected. Call `preventDefault()` to keep the menu open.

parts.description: Contains the navigation menu items and coordinates composite keyboard focus between triggers.
The shared container for the active item's content.

outputs.name: escapeKeyDown
outputs.description: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: focusOutside
outputs.description: Event handler called when focus moves outside the popup. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: pointerDownOutside
outputs.description: Event handler called when a pointerdown event happens outside the popup. Can be prevented.

parts.description:
Structural directive that teleports the navigation menu popup into a container (default
`document.body`) while the menu is open, and keeps it mounted until any CSS exit `@keyframes`
finishes. Set `[keepMounted]="true"` to keep the portal mounted while closed.

Apply it with the `*` microsyntax on the positioner —
`<div *rdxNavigationMenuPortal rdxNavigationMenuPositioner>` — or as an explicit
`<ng-template rdxNavigationMenuPortal>`. For a custom container use the explicit form with
`[container]`.

inputs.name: keepMounted
inputs.description: Keep the portal mounted while the menu is closed.

parts.description:
Dev-mode guard: `rdxNavigationMenuPortal` used to be an attribute directive on a `<div>`. It is now
structural, so the old `<div rdxNavigationMenuPortal>` markup would silently stop portaling — fail
loudly instead.

Positions the shared popup against the active trigger.

A "thin" positioner (ADR 0012): it inherits the popper positioning surface (inputs, `placed`
output, unified vars + placement attrs) from {@link RdxPopperContentWrapper} and adds the
navigation-menu defaults, the open/closed/instant state attributes, and the grace-area hover
bridge. It exposes no legacy `--radix-*` aliases.

inputs.name: align
inputs.description: How to align the popup relative to the specified side. May change when collisions occur.

inputs.name: alignOffset
inputs.description:
An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options. Also accepts an
{@link OffsetFunction} (same signature as `sideOffset`).

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.description: An element to position the popup against. Defaults to the popper anchor.

inputs.name: arrowPadding
inputs.description:
Minimum distance to maintain between the arrow and the edges of the popup.
If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

inputs.name: avoidCollisions
inputs.description:
When `true`, overrides the `side` and `align` preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

`false` disables all avoidance (unless a `collisionAvoidance` object is provided, which wins).

inputs.name: collisionAvoidance
inputs.description:
How the popup avoids colliding with the boundary edges, per axis (Base UI `collisionAvoidance`).
Overrides the deprecated `avoidCollisions` and any per-primitive preset. An object here **fully
replaces** the preset — omitted fields fall back to `side: 'flip'`, `align: 'flip'`,
`fallbackAxisSide: 'end'`, never to the preset. See {@link RdxCollisionAvoidance}.

inputs.name: collisionBoundary
inputs.description:
The element used as the collision boundary.
By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

inputs.name: collisionPadding
inputs.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur.
Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: `{ top: 20, left: 20 }`.

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description:
Text direction, used only to resolve a logical `side` (`inline-start` / `inline-end`) to a
physical one. Falls back to an enclosing direction provider, then `ltr`.

inputs.name: hideWhenDetached
inputs.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

inputs.name: positionStrategy
inputs.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

inputs.name: side
inputs.description:
The preferred side of the anchor to render against when open.
Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

Accepts the logical `'inline-start'` / `'inline-end'` in addition to the physical sides; those
resolve to `left` / `right` for the text direction (Base UI parity). The placed side reported via
`data-side` / `placedSide()` echoes the requested kind: logical in → logical out (even after a
collision flip), physical in → physical out.

inputs.name: sideOffset
inputs.description:
Distance between the anchor and the popup in pixels. Also accepts an {@link OffsetFunction} that
reads the anchor / positioner dimensions and the resolved side / align.

inputs.name: sticky
inputs.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the
content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially
in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary
regardless.

inputs.name: updatePositionStrategy
inputs.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

outputs.name: placed
outputs.description: Emits when the element is placed.

parts.description:
Groups all parts of the navigation menu.

Holds the shared open state: `value` identifies the currently open item, and the menu is open
whenever `value` is non-null. A single popup (Portal → Positioner → Popup → Viewport) is shared
between every item and anchored to the active trigger.

inputs.name: closeDelay
inputs.description: How long to wait before closing the menu after the pointer leaves, in milliseconds.

inputs.name: defaultValue
inputs.description: The uncontrolled value of the item that should be initially open.

inputs.name: delay
inputs.description: How long to wait before opening the menu on hover, in milliseconds.

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description: The reading direction of the navigation menu.

inputs.name: orientation
inputs.description: The orientation of the navigation menu.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The value of the navigation menu item that should be currently open.

outputs.name: onOpenChange
outputs.description: Emits whenever the menu opens or closes.

outputs.name: onOpenChangeComplete
outputs.description: Emits after any enter/exit transition completes.

outputs.name: onValueChange
outputs.description: Emits when the open item changes.

parts.description: A button that opens its item's content in the shared popup.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the trigger should ignore user interaction.

inputs.name: openOnHover
inputs.description: Whether the content should also open when the trigger is hovered.

parts.description:
Clips and animates the active item's content. A single viewport is shared between all items; when
the active item changes the outgoing content is retained as `data-previous` until its CSS
animation/transition completes, and the new content is marked `data-current`.

inputs.name: forceMount
inputs.description: Keep the viewport mounted even when the menu is closed.
